speaker: KING
text: My life itself, and the best heart of it,

speaker: KING
text: Thanks you for this great care. I stood i’ th’ level

speaker: KING
text: Of a full-charged confederacy, and give thanks

speaker: KING
text: To you that choked it.—Let be called before us

speaker: KING
text: That gentleman of Buckingham’s; in person

speaker: KING
text: I’ll hear him his confessions justify,

speaker: KING
text: And point by point the treasons of his master

speaker: KING
text: He shall again relate.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Nay, we must longer kneel; I am a suitor.

speaker: KING
text: Arise, and take place by us.

speaker: KING
text: Half your suit

speaker: KING
text: Never name to us; you have half our power.

speaker: KING
text: The other moiety ere you ask is given;

speaker: KING
text: Repeat your will, and take it.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Thank your Majesty.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: That you would love yourself, and in that love

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Not unconsidered leave your honor nor

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: The dignity of your office, is the point

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Of my petition.

speaker: KING
text: Lady mine, proceed.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I am solicited, not by a few,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: And those of true condition, that your subjects

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Are in great grievance. There have been commissions

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Sent down among ’em which hath flawed the heart

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Of all their loyalties, wherein, although

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: My good Lord Cardinal, they vent reproaches

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Most bitterly on you as putter-on

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Of these exactions, yet the King our master,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Whose honor heaven shield from soil, even he escapes not

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Language unmannerly—yea, such which breaks

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: The sides of loyalty and almost appears

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: In loud rebellion.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Not “almost appears”—

speaker: NORFOLK
text: It doth appear. For, upon these taxations,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The clothiers all, not able to maintain

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The many to them longing, have put off

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The spinsters, carders, fullers, weavers, who,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Unfit for other life, compelled by hunger

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And lack of other means, in desperate manner

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Daring th’ event to th’ teeth, are all in uproar,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And danger serves among them.

speaker: KING
text: Taxation?

speaker: KING
text: Wherein? And what taxation? My Lord Cardinal,

speaker: KING
text: You that are blamed for it alike with us,

speaker: KING
text: Know you of this taxation?

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Please you, sir,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I know but of a single part in aught

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Pertains to th’ state, and front but in that file

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Where others tell steps with me.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: No, my lord?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: You know no more than others? But you frame

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Things that are known alike, which are not wholesome

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: To those which would not know them, and yet must

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Perforce be their acquaintance. These exactions

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Whereof my sovereign would have note, they are

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Most pestilent to th’ hearing, and to bear ’em

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: The back is sacrifice to th’ load. They say

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: They are devised by you, or else you suffer

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Too hard an exclamation.

speaker: KING
text: Still exaction!

speaker: KING
text: The nature of it? In what kind, let’s know,

speaker: KING
text: Is this exaction?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I am much too venturous

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: In tempting of your patience, but am boldened

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Under your promised pardon. The subjects’ grief

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Comes through commissions which compels from each

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: The sixth part of his substance, to be levied

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Without delay, and the pretense for this

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Is named your wars in France. This makes bold mouths.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Tongues spit their duties out, and cold hearts freeze

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Allegiance in them. Their curses now

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Live where their prayers did; and it’s come to pass

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: This tractable obedience is a slave

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: To each incensèd will. I would your Highness

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Would give it quick consideration, for

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: There is no primer baseness.

speaker: KING
text: By my life,

speaker: KING
text: This is against our pleasure.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And for me,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I have no further gone in this than by

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A single voice, and that not passed me but

speaker: WOLSEY
text: By learnèd approbation of the judges. If I am

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Traduced by ignorant tongues, which neither know

speaker: WOLSEY
text: My faculties nor person, yet will be

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The chronicles of my doing, let me say

speaker: WOLSEY
text: ’Tis but the fate of place, and the rough brake

speaker: WOLSEY
text: That virtue must go through. We must not stint

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Our necessary actions in the fear

speaker: WOLSEY
text: To cope malicious censurers, which ever,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: As ravenous fishes, do a vessel follow

speaker: WOLSEY
text: That is new trimmed, but benefit no further

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Than vainly longing. What we oft do best,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: By sick interpreters, once weak ones, is

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Not ours or not allowed; what worst, as oft,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Hitting a grosser quality, is cried up

speaker: WOLSEY
text: For our best act. If we shall stand still

speaker: WOLSEY
text: In fear our motion will be mocked or carped at,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: We should take root here where we sit,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Or sit state-statues only.

speaker: KING
text: Things done well,

speaker: KING
text: And with a care, exempt themselves from fear;

speaker: KING
text: Things done without example, in their issue

speaker: KING
text: Are to be feared. Have you a precedent

speaker: KING
text: Of this commission? I believe, not any.

speaker: KING
text: We must not rend our subjects from our laws

speaker: KING
text: And stick them in our will. Sixth part of each?

speaker: KING
text: A trembling contribution! Why, we take

speaker: KING
text: From every tree lop, bark, and part o’ th’ timber,

speaker: KING
text: And though we leave it with a root, thus hacked,

speaker: KING
text: The air will drink the sap. To every county

speaker: KING
text: Where this is questioned send our letters with

speaker: KING
text: Free pardon to each man that has denied

speaker: KING
text: The force of this commission. Pray look to ’t;

speaker: KING
text: I put it to your care.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A word with you.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Let there be letters writ to every shire

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Of the King’s grace and pardon. The grievèd commons

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Hardly conceive of me. Let it be noised

speaker: WOLSEY
text: That through our intercession this revokement

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And pardon comes. I shall anon advise you

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Further in the proceeding.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I am sorry that the Duke of Buckingham

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Is run in your displeasure.

speaker: KING
text: It grieves many.

speaker: KING
text: The gentleman is learnèd and a most rare speaker;

speaker: KING
text: To nature none more bound; his training such

speaker: KING
text: That he may furnish and instruct great teachers

speaker: KING
text: And never seek for aid out of himself. Yet see,

speaker: KING
text: When these so noble benefits shall prove

speaker: KING
text: Not well disposed, the mind growing once corrupt,

speaker: KING
text: They turn to vicious forms ten times more ugly

speaker: KING
text: Than ever they were fair. This man so complete,

speaker: KING
text: Who was enrolled ’mongst wonders, and when we

speaker: KING
text: Almost with ravished list’ning could not find

speaker: KING
text: His hour of speech a minute—he, my lady,

speaker: KING
text: Hath into monstrous habits put the graces

speaker: KING
text: That once were his, and is become as black

speaker: KING
text: As if besmeared in hell. Sit by us. You shall hear—

speaker: KING
text: This was his gentleman in trust—of him

speaker: KING
text: Things to strike honor sad.—Bid him recount

speaker: KING
text: The fore-recited practices, whereof

speaker: KING
text: We cannot feel too little, hear too much.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Stand forth, and with bold spirit relate what you

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Most like a careful subject have collected

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Out of the Duke of Buckingham.

speaker: KING
text: Speak freely.

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: First, it was usual with him—every day

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: It would infect his speech—that if the King

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Should without issue die, he’ll carry it so

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: To make the scepter his. These very words

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: I’ve heard him utter to his son-in-law,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Lord Abergavenny, to whom by oath he menaced

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Revenge upon the Cardinal.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Please your Highness, note

speaker: WOLSEY
text: This dangerous conception in this point:

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Not friended by his wish to your high person,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: His will is most malignant, and it stretches

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Beyond you to your friends.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: My learnèd Lord Cardinal,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Deliver all with charity.

speaker: KING
text: Speak on.

speaker: KING
text: How grounded he his title to the crown

speaker: KING
text: Upon our fail? To this point hast thou heard him

speaker: KING
text: At any time speak aught?

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: He was brought to this

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: By a vain prophecy of Nicholas Henton.

speaker: KING
text: What was that Henton?

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Sir, a Chartreux friar,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: His confessor, who fed him every minute

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: With words of sovereignty.

speaker: KING
text: How know’st thou this?

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Not long before your Highness sped to France,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: The Duke being at the Rose, within the parish

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Saint Laurence Poultney, did of me demand

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: What was the speech among the Londoners

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Concerning the French journey. I replied

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Men fear the French would prove perfidious,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: To the King’s danger. Presently the Duke

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Said ’twas the fear indeed, and that he doubted

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: ’Twould prove the verity of certain words

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Spoke by a holy monk “that oft,” says he,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: “Hath sent to me, wishing me to permit

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: John de la Car, my chaplain, a choice hour

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: To hear from him a matter of some moment;

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Whom after under the confession’s seal

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: He solemnly had sworn that what he spoke

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: My chaplain to no creature living but

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: To me should utter, with demure confidence

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: This pausingly ensued: ‘Neither the King, nor ’s heirs—

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Tell you the Duke—shall prosper. Bid him strive

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: To gain the love o’ th’ commonalty; the Duke

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Shall govern England.’”

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: If I know you well,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: You were the Duke’s surveyor, and lost your office

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: On the complaint o’ th’ tenants. Take good heed

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: You charge not in your spleen a noble person

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: And spoil your nobler soul. I say, take heed—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Yes, heartily beseech you.

speaker: KING
text: Let him on.—

speaker: KING
text: Go forward.

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: On my soul, I’ll speak but truth.

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: I told my lord the Duke, by th’ devil’s illusions

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: The monk might be deceived, and that ’twas dangerous

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: For him to ruminate on this so far until

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: It forged him some design, which, being believed,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: It was much like to do. He answered “Tush,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: It can do me no damage,” adding further

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: That had the King in his last sickness failed,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: The Cardinal’s and Sir Thomas Lovell’s heads

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Should have gone off.

speaker: KING
text: Ha! What, so rank? Ah ha!

speaker: KING
text: There’s mischief in this man! Canst thou say further?

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: I can, my liege.

speaker: KING
text: Proceed.

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Being at Greenwich,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: After your Highness had reproved the Duke

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: About Sir William Blumer—

speaker: KING
text: I remember of such a time, being my sworn servant,

speaker: KING
text: The Duke retained him his. But on. What hence?

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: “If,” quoth he, “I for this had been committed,”

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: As to the Tower, I thought, “I would have played

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: The part my father meant to act upon

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Th’ usurper Richard, who, being at Salisbury,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Made suit to come in ’s presence; which if granted,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: As he made semblance of his duty, would

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Have put his knife into him.”

speaker: KING
text: A giant traitor!

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Now, madam, may his Highness live in freedom

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And this man out of prison?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: God mend all.

speaker: KING
text: There’s something more would out of thee. What sayst?

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: After “the Duke his father” with “the knife,”

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: He stretched him, and with one hand on his dagger,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Another spread on ’s breast, mounting his eyes,

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: He did discharge a horrible oath whose tenor

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Was, were he evil used, he would outgo

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: His father by as much as a performance

speaker: SURVEYOR
text: Does an irresolute purpose.

speaker: KING
text: There’s his period,

speaker: KING
text: To sheathe his knife in us! He is attached.

speaker: KING
text: Call him to present trial. If he may

speaker: KING
text: Find mercy in the law, ’tis his; if none,

speaker: KING
text: Let him not seek ’t of us. By day and night,

speaker: KING
text: He’s traitor to th’ height!
